skill.name: nature-reader
skill.description: Creates source-grounded bilingual Markdown readers for academic papers from PDFs, DOI or arXiv links, publisher HTML, and pasted text. It preserves original text, Chinese translations, source anchors, figures, tables, captions, and extraction notes.
skill.summary: Create bilingual, figure-aware Markdown readers for academic papers.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: AI review downgraded the static risk score because the command, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and entropy alerts are documentation false positives. The confirmed issue is a medium filesystem concern: the skill instructs agents to load shared files through parent-directory paths, which can cross package boundaries if not isolated. No prompt injection, executable scripts, credential access, or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Parent-Directory Fragment Loading
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to load shared files with parent-directory paths such as ../_shared and ../../../_shared. This is not malicious by itself, but it can cross package boundaries and load unreviewed content if marketplace isolation is weak.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact parent-directory paths are present in routing and workflow instructions. The referenced target appears to be shared terminology content, so the risk is package-boundary exposure rather than confirmed malicious activity.

low_findings.title: Identifier Resolution May Use Network Access
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The DOI and arXiv fragment directs the agent to resolve identifiers to article pages and open-access PDFs or HTML. This is a legitimate research workflow, but it should be disclosed because network retrieval can contact external publisher or preprint services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe resolving DOI or arXiv identifiers and extracting article HTML. No credential handling, webhook, or exfiltration endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged many inline backticks and fenced Markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed files are Markdown instructions and examples, not executable Ruby code, and no shell command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context review shows the flagged characters mark filenames, IDs, and Markdown code fences. There is no Ruby source file or instruction to execute a backticked shell command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur in prose, Markdown file names, or examples. No hashing, encryption, signature verification, or password handling code was found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains Markdown and YAML guidance, not cryptographic routines. The flagged lines do not implement or recommend MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithms.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance findings appear in descriptive academic-reading text. No commands gather hostnames, users, processes, environment data, network interfaces, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found ordinary documentation around article structure and output rules. No reconnaissance tool names or system data collection workflow was present.

low_findings.title: Entropy Alerts Match Natural Language Test Prompts
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-entropy findings in evals/evals.json are Chinese evaluation prompts and expected outputs. The README entropy finding is also natural-language Markdown, not encoded payload material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged eval lines are readable Chinese prompts and expected behavior descriptions. No base64 blob, compressed payload, encrypted string, or decoder logic was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent-Directory Fragment Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent to load shared files with parent-directory paths such as ../_shared and ../../../_shared. This is not malicious by itself, but it can cross package boundaries and load unreviewed content if marketplace isolation is weak.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact parent-directory paths are present in routing and workflow instructions. The referenced target appears to be shared terminology content, so the risk is package-boundary exposure rather than confirmed malicious activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Identifier Resolution May Use Network Access
dangerous_patterns.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The DOI and arXiv fragment directs the agent to resolve identifiers to article pages and open-access PDFs or HTML. This is a legitimate research workflow, but it should be disclosed because network retrieval can contact external publisher or preprint services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe resolving DOI or arXiv identifiers and extracting article HTML. No credential handling, webhook, or exfiltration endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Research papers are hard to study when translation, figures, captions, and source locations are separated. This skill creates a full Markdown reading artifact with original text, Chinese translation, visual assets, and traceable anchors.
actual_capabilities: Builds paragraph-level Original and Chinese pairs in a paper.md file.
Creates stable page and block anchors for source-grounded follow-up answers.
Guides extraction and placement of figures, tables, and bilingual captions.
Handles selectable PDFs, scanned PDFs, publisher HTML, DOI or arXiv links, and pasted text.
Records uncertain OCR, missing pages, missing figures, and draft-mode gaps in translation notes.
Defines a source_map.json structure for blocks, figures, pages, and terminology.
limitations: It depends on available extraction tools for PDF text, OCR, and image cropping.
It cannot lawfully reproduce restricted full text unless the user provides the source or open access is clear.
It may produce draft output when only an abstract, DOI metadata, or partial pasted text is available.
It is a workflow skill, not a standalone parser or executable conversion program.

use_cases.title: Translate a Research Paper
use_cases.description: Turn a user-provided PDF into a complete Chinese-English Markdown reader with paragraph alignment and source anchors.

use_cases.title: Study Figures With Context
use_cases.description: Place cropped figures and tables near the translated prose that first explains them, with bilingual captions attached.

use_cases.title: Prepare Source-Grounded Notes
use_cases.description: Create a traceable reading artifact that supports later questions with page numbers, block IDs, and figure references.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF Reader
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete bilingual Markdown reader.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nature-reader on this PDF. Create paper.md with Original and Chinese pairs, source anchors, and notes for anything uncertain.

prompt_templates.title: Figure-Aware Translation
prompt_templates.scenario: Translate the paper while keeping visuals close to the relevant text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this paper into a Chinese-English Markdown reader. Extract figures and tables into assets, keep bilingual captions, and place each visual near its first substantive mention.

prompt_templates.title: DOI or arXiv Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Start from an identifier instead of a local file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve this DOI or arXiv link first. If full text is available, build the full bilingual reader. If not, mark the missing sections clearly.

prompt_templates.title: OCR and Grounding Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for scanned PDFs and later source-grounded questions.
prompt_templates.prompt: This is a scanned paper. Run OCR-aware extraction, create source_map.json, mark low-confidence text, crop figures, and prepare grounded follow-up citations by page and block ID.

output_examples.input: A selectable-text PDF of a journal article.
output_examples.output: A Markdown reader with metadata, page index, aligned original and Chinese paragraphs, figure cards, captions, source anchors, and translation notes.

output_examples.input: A scanned conference paper with several low-quality figures.
output_examples.output: A draft bilingual reader that labels OCR uncertainty, includes available figure crops, and records pages or visuals that could not be verified.

output_examples.input: A DOI for a paper where only the abstract is reachable.
output_examples.output: A draft reader based on confirmed abstract and metadata, with explicit notes that the body text was not retrieved.

best_practices: Provide the PDF or a lawful open-access link when asking for full-text translation.
Ask for draft mode when the paper is long, scanned, or only partly available.
Review translation_notes.md before relying on OCR-heavy passages or figure crops.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a short summary or citation lookup.
Do not ask it to invent missing sections, figures, or page numbers.
Do not paste restricted full text into chat when a local artifact is the safer output.

faq.question: Does this skill only work for Nature papers?
faq.answer: No. It supports journal papers, preprints, conference papers, and pasted academic text across disciplines.

faq.question: What is the main output?
faq.answer: The main output is paper.md with original text, Chinese translation, source anchors, figures, captions, and notes.

faq.question: Can it process scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, if OCR tooling is available. It should mark uncertain OCR blocks instead of guessing.

faq.question: Can it start from a DOI or arXiv link?
faq.answer: Yes. It first resolves the identifier, then builds a reader from the reachable lawful source.

faq.question: Does it create summaries?
faq.answer: It can include short reading notes, but it is designed for full bilingual readers, not summary-only output.

faq.question: How does it handle copyright limits?
faq.answer: It keeps chat responses short for restricted sources and points to local artifacts when the user supplied the file.
